description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden canopy roof. The roof is supported by two sturdy wooden posts and features a red, tiled, slightly curved design, giving it a rustic appearance. The well has a visible wooden crank handle and a horizontal spindle, suggesting a manual water-drawing mechanism, though no bucket is present. The stonework is uneven and stylized, with a few loose stones scattered around the base, adding to its aged, hand-built look. The wood appears weathered, with visible grain and darkened edges, while the stone is rough and gray. The overall structure is compact and robust, suitable for placement in parks, courtyards, or near urban walkways.
description: A rustic stone water well with a red-tiled wooden canopy and a manual crank mechanism, featuring weathered wood and rough stonework.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red-tiled roof, stone well opening, and crank handle partially visible beneath the canopy.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved wooden roof structure and the full profile of the stone well base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the roof and stonework visible but the crank handle less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank handle and spindle mechanism more clearly, along with the stone base and canopy details.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden crank and spindle mechanism is present, mounted horizontally between the posts, but no bucket is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a circular stone wall approximately waist-high, with a wooden canopy supported by two posts.
attribute_decorative_elements: Carved wooden details on the canopy ends.
